title: racosperma neriifolium subsp. pustulum
intro: Racosperma neriifolium subsp. pustulum, also known as the Pustulum Acacia, is a species of shrub in the family Fabaceae. It is a plant native to Western Australia, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as arthritis and skin infections. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, including infusions and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and fever.
